oxide_basic.description: Basic oxides: oxides of metals with oxidation state +1 or +2 (except amphoteric ones). React with acids and acidic oxides.

oxide_acidic.description: Acidic oxides: oxides of nonmetals (except CO, NO, N₂O) and metal oxides with high oxidation state (+5, +6, +7). React with bases and basic oxides.

oxide_amphoteric.description: Amphoteric oxides: oxides of amphoteric metals. React with both acids and alkalis.

oxide_indifferent.description: Non-salt-forming oxides: do not form salts when interacting with acids or bases.

acid_oxygen_containing.description: Oxyacids: contain oxygen in the acid residue.

acid_oxygen_free.description: Binary acids: do not contain oxygen. Formed by hydrogen and a nonmetal.

base_soluble.description: Soluble bases (alkalis): hydroxides of alkali and alkaline earth metals. Soluble in water, dissociate producing OH⁻ ions.

base_insoluble.description: Insoluble bases: hydroxides of heavy metals. Do not dissolve in water, decompose upon heating.

base_amphoteric.description: Amphoteric hydroxides: exhibit both acidic and basic properties. React with both acids and alkalis.

salt_normal.description: Normal (neutral) salts: product of complete replacement of hydrogen atoms in an acid by metal cations (or NH₄⁺).

salt_acidic.description: Acidic salts: product of incomplete replacement of hydrogen atoms in a polybasic acid. Contain hydrogen ion in the acid residue.

salt_basic.description: Basic salts: product of incomplete replacement of hydroxide ions in a polyacidic base. Contain a hydroxo group.
